Description: Tune the Tokio runtime for your workload type: I/O-bound vs CPU-bound, worker threads vs blocking threads
Do: Use multi_thread for servers; current_thread for simple tools; configure worker_threads; set max_blocking_threads
Dont: Use default runtime without considering workload; start multiple runtimes
Code Bad: let rt = tokio::runtime::Runtime::new().unwrap();\nrt.block_on(async { /* ... */ });\n// inside a function that's called many times = multiple runtimes

Guideline: Use JoinSet for managing task groups
Description: JoinSet provides clean management of spawned task groups with result collection
Do: Use JoinSet for dynamic task sets; call join_next() to collect results; drop JoinSet to cancel all tasks
Dont: Use Vec<JoinHandle> and manually join all; forget to handle panicked tasks

Guideline: Use tokio::time for delays and intervals
Description: Use Tokio's time utilities for non-blocking delays, intervals, and timeouts
Do: Use tokio::time::sleep instead of std::thread::sleep; use interval for periodic tasks; use timeout for deadlines
Dont: Use std::thread::sleep in async code; busy-loop for timing

Guideline: Use tokio::fs and tokio::io for async I/O
Description: Tokio provides async versions of file and I/O operations that do not block the runtime
Do: Use tokio::fs for file operations; tokio::io for streams; BufReader/BufWriter from tokio::io
Dont: Use std::fs in async functions which blocks the Tokio worker thread

Description: Use tokio-console to inspect running tasks, their poll times, and resource usage in real-time
Do: Add console-subscriber as a dependency; enable tracing instrumentation; connect with tokio-console CLI
Dont: Rely solely on log output for debugging async issues; use println for timing
